text: Management is very unprofessional. DJ is unprofessional, and  Maurice who is supposedly the General Manager. I’ve watched fights and arguments happen in the dish room and kitchen, item stolen, and instead of management sending aggressive individuals home, they are kept, so these individuals feel comfortable walking around and bullying other workers. When I reported to the head chef Brandon, Chif, Maurice, and DJ that a individual yelled at me and was aggressive to avoid workplace violence, I was told I was being “sensitive”. But if you engage/ defend yourself in a disagreement, it’s a problem, but they allow workplace bullying, harassment and violence The head chefs are overly loud and disrespectful, using moments to belittle instead of teaching. A VERY hostile work environment. There are roaches, it’s dirty and overall a bad work environment and i wouldn’t recommend. This location needs to be looked into and management should be improved. title: West Village Dining Commons

name: Safeer Mithani
text: West Village Dining Hall is one of the worst places I have ever eaten in my life. It is unfortunate that it is the closest dining hall to my apartment. I found a grasshopper in my bok choi. Ew. Additionally, the food consistently lacks taste, and their lack of tasty vegetarian options dissuades me from going there. I avoid it like the plague. West Village Dining Hall is one of the biggest challenges to freshman students living on the west side of campus who are trying to achieve a healthy lifestyle. Children who say “yuck, vegetables are gross,” have probably been exposed to West Village at some point in their lives. This is not slander; this is a reality. It makes me not only physically unwell, but it sometimes impacts my mental health. I often feel sad and disappointed by the amount of food waste that I see. I have tried eating the food there. I rarely finish it. It tastes awful. I feel guilty for having to toss it every time, so now I just avoid it. For students who might not have the time and energy to travel to the east side of campus where healthy food is more readily available, west village may represent the only option. From undercooked chicken to grasshoppers in bok choi to pork labeled as chicken (a landmine for Muslims like me), west village has so little to offer to its customers. I would highly recommend those students to invest in alternative forms of transportation like bicycles so that they can both get exercise as well as a quick, convenient escape from west village to Brittain and North Avenue dining halls, as well as other on-campus dining locations. The only good thing about this place is that the bakery/coffee stand makes pretty decent pastries as well as the fact that it tends to have more fresh fruit than the other dining halls.

Then, when you finally get up to order, they rarely have anything you want and it’s poor quality anyway. Waaaaaay too much oil is used in the cooking (pasta,OMLETTES,vegetables) as either an attempt to cover up the poor quality food or, better yet, the lack of desire to produce healthy good tasting meals.

name: Ben Bogard
text: Oh the 5-star reviews are so obviously fake ahaha! As a student at Tech eating mealplan every day I visit Willage on a daily basis. Not ONCE have I received a meal that was "good" on a good day the food is at best, "palatable" and at its worst disgusting. The staff are rude and berate you for wanting two swipes of food (as portions are skimpy especially meat wise) and the ALWAYS run out!! For some reason almost a year into my time at Tech they consistently run out of: dessert, tortellini, salad, anything at Local Chef for dinner, parfaits, cheese bagels, bagels in general, and so much more. Sodexo is the problem here! GaTech needs to LISTEN to the students rather than sign more contracts with prison/hospital food suppliers like Sodexo/Aramark. Good quality food is essential for a properly functioning brain and as Tech claims to be "creating the next" "the next" would likely benefit an increase in quality, nutritious food. Hopefully change comes eventually...

Now, it would be unreasonable to complain if they were making filet mignon steaks cooked when ordered, but it's omelets. OMELETS. How hard can it be? Also, the oil to egg ratio is heinous and makes me want to yack sauce in the bathroom. Waited 30 minutes in a line that was 7 people long with three pans to cook omelets in. Also witnessed a girl be denied of a nice parfait because... "we out, that ain't nun my business anyways", when the fridge right behind the worker was slammed full of parfaits. Shall I go on... The food is bad, undercooked or overcooked, a big part of the staff impolite or even rude, lines are long and the tables frequently dirty.

text: If I could give it no stars then I would.  Alumni returning for a visit with old prof pal.  Nice to see on campus food is still just as terrible as when I was a student.  Unfortunately it is also crazy expensive now.  Easily one of the worst meals I have ever eaten.  What is wrong with the people running this place?  Truly horrible, disgusting and over priced.  GT Admin should be ashamed.
title: West Village Dining Commons

name: Sam Wiley
text: I have yet to have a fully positive experience here after eating here for over a year now. The prices are wild (two dollars for an eggroll, 1 dollars for a 16 ounce drink, which is higher than I've seen at any other restaurant, not to mention the fact that the cups that they hand out are probably 8 ounces max, not the listed 160z), the lines are ridiculously long, the employees are almost always rude or angry with you, they're almost always out of many of the options, of which there are not many to begin with. There are not many healthy options like vegetables, unless you intend to buy a very small, overpriced, salad. The quality of the food is low, with it often being overcooked or undercooked, and you can sit and watch the employees take out massive bags of frozen foods and dump them into vats to heat for serving. Not that that is surprising from a college dining hall, but at the prices they charge, it is quite outrageous.  Whenever I have time, I walk all the way to the other side of campus to eat in one of the other two dining halls and my experience is always infinitely better, from the salad bar, to the ice cream machine, and the many different choices they have every night. The purpose of a college dining hall is to provide food for the students, not pad a profit margin, but it seems like West Village never got that message.
title: West Village Dining Commons

name: CJ Futch
text: This place is hit or miss. Sometimes the food is great, prepared on time, and by amazingly nice workers. Other times, though, the food is terrible, the lines are extremely long, the workers are rude, your order is messed up, and the restaurants are out of everything. It's normally the latter. Especially running out of supplies. I have literally never been to West Village without them being out of something that I went there with the expectation of getting. Be that utensils, the meal I was intending to order, various beverages, various condiments, napkins, or hand sanitizer. If it is a commodity, they will run out of it before it is restocked.

name: Grant Champagne
text: I like this new version of food GT Dining is moving towards, away from mediocre, buffet style food and towards better, to-order meals. The building itself is very nice, as are the Starbucks and Panera locations within. However, the 5 GT Dining operated "microrestaurants" are run poorly, like the other dining halls. Drinks, condiments, and certain items on the menu are routinely out of stock, and the lines can be suffocatingly long. Service is horrible and the staff  is rude, with a few exceptions. I like the idea and the food, but ultimately it is still poorly run and managed by the same incompetent people.
title: West Village Dining Commons

name: Jerry
text:
tldr: sodexo screwed over the students on West campus with West Village due to their greed. DO NOT purchase a meal plan if you are on west campus unless you want small portions, overpriced options, semi-burnt food, long wait times for food, running out of certain options, etc. Honestly don't purchase a meal plan even if you're not on West campus so you don't support such a greedy deceitful company like sodexo. I'd give 0 stars if I could.

I am a 4th year student who has purchased the unlimited meal plan every semester that I have been at Gatech because I was happy with the community restaurants. However, closing down Woodruff dining hall along with Westside market for West Village is the worst thing that has happened to Georgia Tech since I started Fall 2014. A lot of portions are too small, many of the options are insanely overpriced (ex: $7.49 for some noodles and chicken, $1.99 for some leaves of lettuce with sauce on top aka mixed greens with ginger sauce), the wait time to get food was increased dramatically compared to Woodys, meal swipes are under priced and you can't mix options at different stations with the same swipe, many of the options contain semi-burnt food, the food is sometimes the same but most of the time worse quality than it was at Woodys which makes absolutely no sense, and I can keep going on about all of the problems with West Village. Gatech Dining services did not make it clear at all that Woodys was going to be shut down.

The ONLY reason I purchased a meal plan this semester is because I thought Woodys would be open in conjunction with West Village. The only positive thing about West Village is the hours of operation are a bit better, but even this is offset by the fact that they didn't have the option that I wanted when I was there a lot of the time. I tried to get a partial refund of my meal plan by emailing a rep from sodexo but they are probably ignoring my email because I found out that meal plan purchases are non-refundable. I will definitely not purchase a meal plan next semester and I advise anyone in an apartment to never get a meal plan with the hopes of using west village. So thank you Gatech dining services and sodexo for deceiving and screwing over the people on West campus. Gatech needs to drop sodexo asap before their greed screws over the students on East campus as well.
title: West Village Dining Commons

name: Silver Arrow
text: The employees are very sweet and some even know me by name. The food, however, is another story. Nearly all of the meals are very heavy and oily. There isn’t much access to fresh vegetables unless you want a salad every night. My roommate and I have each had food poisoning and stomach upsets many times after eating there. Not sure if they’re not sanitary enough or if the ingredients are just low quality and not prepared well.
